title: Hemionitis chrysophylla
taxonomy: Hemionitis chrysophylla is classified in the family Polypodiaceae and is a type of species within the genus Hemionitis. This genus is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes various species of ferns. The Polypodiales order is further classified within the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. This classification places Hemionitis chrysophylla within the broader group of vascular plants.

intro: Hemionitis chrysophylla, also known as the golden-leaved hemionitis, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its shiny, golden-green fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis chrysophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Hemionitis chrysophylla is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture to treat various health conditions, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceutical formulations.
